loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

summary: Accepted CodeWiki alignment hardening principles: graph/gateway/content-proof precedence, audit engine plus /audit, semantic build traceability, universal feedback classification, dogfood/source path taxonomy, gate-required audit profiles, publication as content-proof layer, and sprint decomposition.

diff_table.current_state: Graph is a generated status/index surface with partial reconciliation rules; green graph can miss vertical drift.
diff_table.desired_state: Graph becomes derived vertical alignment state machine with executable layer-edge invariants. Graph is required gateway input but never overrides canonical sources or immutable content proofs.
diff_table.rationale: Vertical drift prevention requires graph invariants plus clear precedence: graph routes/summarizes; canonical sources/content proofs prove; gateways decide.

diff_table.current_state: Compiler builds and validation gateways exist, but policies do not require complete horizontal alignment evidence for every build class or closure gate.
diff_table.desired_state: Compilers propagate intent and create build handoffs; gateways independently verify horizontal and cross-layer consistency using graph context, canonical sources, audit evidence, and content proofs. Gateway reports are attestations, not content proof.
diff_table.rationale: Builds are handoff evidence; gateways are independent judgment; commits/tree SHAs/package digests anchor actual content.

diff_table.current_state: Auditing is mostly ad hoc and could fragment into many user commands.
diff_table.desired_state: Create one user command `/audit [flags]` backed by one internal `codewiki_audit` engine. No flags means full audit; flags select profile/scope. Gateways call same internal engine with policy-selected profiles.
diff_table.rationale: One engine avoids duplicate logic and supports both user observability and machine-readable gateway evidence.

diff_table.current_state: Build traceability is expected but not strict enough for all semantic changes.
diff_table.desired_state: Every semantic change must trace to an accepted compiler build before close, validation, or publication. Generated/runtime/mechanical changes do not need their own build unless semantic, but attach to the relevant change set.

diff_table.current_state: Agent-led implementation can bypass feedback when framed as bugfix, cleanup, maintenance, or code work.
diff_table.desired_state: All semantic changes enter feedback classification, then route as product, system/architecture, task, code bugfix, maintenance/audit, security, or publication changes with explicit propagation rules.
diff_table.rationale: User-agent interaction is first truth layer; classification prevents code-first changes skipping docs/roadmap/tests.

diff_table.current_state: Dogfood CodeWiki state and package source share one repo; prior prompts/docs/checks did not fully enforce the boundary.
diff_table.desired_state: Define executable path taxonomy: product source, dogfood canonical state, generated graph/views, transient builds, runtime/session state, tests, scripts/dev tooling, package/publication files. In this repo `.codewiki/` is dogfood; source/package lives outside it.
diff_table.rationale: File-structure drift happened partly because agents could confuse CodeWiki product source with CodeWiki dogfood wiki.

diff_table.current_state: Validation can pass on typecheck/tests while missing file-ownership, stale reference, package reachability, security, or generated/canonical drift.
diff_table.desired_state: Add gate-required audit profiles: file-structure, stale-reference, package-reachability, security/dependency, generated-vs-canonical parity, and layer-alignment. Profiles emit machine-readable evidence for graph/gateways.
diff_table.rationale: Generic checks are necessary but not enough; known drift classes need detectors.

diff_table.current_state: Publication/commits are not consistently modeled as alignment layer or content proof.
diff_table.desired_state: Treat commits, tree SHAs, package tarball digests, PR descriptions, release notes, archive ledgers, and safe-to-push evidence as publication/content-proof nodes. Pre-commit validation may use working-tree diff/tree digest; publication requires immutable commit/package proof.
diff_table.rationale: Validation is judgment; commit/tree/package digest is stronger proof of what exists or shipped.

diff_table.current_state: TASK-077 is broad; solving drift needs a detailed sprint split by architectural track.
diff_table.desired_state: Create sprint tasks for alignment model spec, graph reconciliation v2, gateway policy profiles, audit engine/command, dogfood boundary hardening, change classification, regression fixtures, and publication alignment.
diff_table.rationale: Cross-cutting work must be split to avoid another opaque refactor and hidden drift.

accepted_decisions.summary: Use 'Change' terminology for feedback table rows instead of DTR.
Graph is required gateway input and vertical state-machine substrate, but canonical sources and immutable content proofs have precedence.
Validation reports are attestations; commits/tree SHAs/package digests are content proofs.
Expose one `/audit [flags]` command backed by one internal `codewiki_audit` engine used by gateways.
Harden traceability: every semantic change must link to an accepted compiler build before close/validate/publish.
All semantic agent-led changes enter feedback classification before propagation.
Plan TASK-077 as a sprint of focused tasks, not one opaque refactor.

knowledge: Document alignment model, graph/gateway/content-proof precedence, audit engine/command, semantic build traceability, change classification, path taxonomy, publication layer.
roadmap: Create sprint tasks under TASK-077 for model, graph, gateways, audit, boundary, classification, regression fixtures, publication alignment.
code: Future code changes will add codewiki_audit, gateway policy hooks, graph reconciliation rules, architecture/package/security/stale-ref checks, and tests.

knowledge: Document alignment model, graph/gateway/content-proof precedence, audit engine/command, semantic build traceability, change classification, path taxonomy, publication layer.
roadmap: Create sprint tasks under TASK-077 for model, graph, gateways, audit, boundary, classification, regression fixtures, publication alignment.
code: Future code changes will add codewiki_audit, gateway policy hooks, graph reconciliation rules, architecture/package/security/stale-ref checks, and tests.
